title: QUIC Connection Runtime Endpoint Ingress Architecture
purpose: Describe the fixed endpoint-side ingress pipeline, endpoint-owned routing/token registries, and explicit effect handoff that deliver datagrams to the correct runtime without mutating connection-owned state inline.
design_summary: The endpoint owns a fixed ingress classifier that parses the packet form, resolves long-header routes by exact destination CID, resolves short-header routes by registered CID-prefix length, and only then screens for stateless reset. A stable connection handle stays on the host side, while route and reset-token registries remain endpoint-owned and concurrent. Explicit effects update endpoint bindings and retire route or token ownership so the connection runtime never mutates shared ingress state inline.
risks: Short-header prefix matching must stay deterministic when multiple CID lengths are registered.
Reset-token bindings must follow the current remote address for a handle or matching can drift after path changes.
Discard effects must remove both route and token ownership to avoid stale reuse.

supplemental_sections.heading: Ingress Flow
supplemental_sections.content:
1. Classify the datagram header form.
2. Handle version negotiation and retry at the endpoint boundary.
3. Route live traffic by destination connection ID or registered CID prefix.
4. Screen route misses for stateless-reset eligibility.
5. Post explicit connection events for routed traffic or accepted resets.

supplemental_sections.heading: Registry Model
supplemental_sections.content:
The endpoint maintains concurrent route and stateless-reset registries keyed by stable connection handles.
Connection-owned runtime state never owns those maps directly.
Binding updates refresh the endpoint view of the current remote address so reset matching stays scoped to the live route.

supplemental_sections.heading: Effect Bridge
supplemental_sections.content:
Endpoint binding updates, reset-token registration, reset-token retirement, and discard cleanup are applied through explicit effects.
That keeps the connection runtime single-owner while still letting the endpoint retire routing state deterministically.
